description_long: This is a round, white cup with a slightly bulbous body and a polygonal handle attached to one side. The surface is marked with a faint blue and red grid pattern, giving it a technical or prototype appearance. On one side, the word 'VICTOR' is printed in large red letters, while the opposite side displays 'WILIS' in the same style. The cup has a wide, circular opening at the top and a flat base for stability. The handle is angular and geometric, contrasting with the otherwise smooth, curved body. The cup appears to be made of ceramic or porcelain, with a matte finish and no visible lid or decorative motifs beyond the text.
description: A white, round cup with a polygonal handle and red text ('VICTOR' and 'WILIS') on opposite sides, featuring a subtle grid pattern.
description_view_0: Front view showing the word 'VICTOR' in red and the polygonal handle on the right.
description_view_1: Side view with the handle centered and no visible text, highlighting the cup's round shape.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the word 'WILIS' in red, with the handle on the left.
description_view_3: Back view showing the plain, curved surface of the cup with no handle or text visible.
